subject: Frontiers Newsletter — August 2016
body:
Frontiers

http://home.frontiersin.org/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_TopBanner 

TOP NEWS - AUGUST 2016

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/21/frontiers-publishes-its-50000th-article/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_50KImage 

Frontiers publishes 50,000th article

Frontiers has reached a major milestone and published its 50,000th article. We are now the home of 57 journals, and the largest editorial board in the world, consisting of over 63,000 researchers from the most prestigious universities. 

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/21/frontiers-publishes-its-50000th-article/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_50KText 
Read more here. 

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/21/frontiers-collaborative-peer-review-quality-at-scale/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_QualityImage 

Achieving quality at scale: here's how we do it

Publishing 50,000 articles is just the beginning. 
http://loop.frontiersin.org/people/68950/bio/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_QualityMarie 
Dr. Marie Souliere  explains how quality at scale is achieved through our unique collaborative peer-review platform. Marie Souliere is the Senior Manager of Peer Review Operations, and of Quality and Ethics at Frontiers.


 
http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/21/frontiers-collaborative-peer-review-quality-at-scale/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_QualityText 
Watch the video and read more here. 

EMPOWERING & SUPPORTING COMMUNITIES

http://vimeo.com/179173750/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_SummitImage 

Science for sustainability: 

Editors Summit Highlights 

Over 40 Frontiers editors came together to discuss the future of academic publishing, and how scientists can play a vital role in tackling the global sustainability crisis at our 2016 Frontiers Editors Summit. This video shows all the highlights of the event from great speeches to interesting discussions, all in front of the backdrop of the beautiful Swiss Alps.

http://vimeo.com/179173750/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_SummitText 
Watch the video here. 

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/17/frontiers-in-medicine-welcomes-new-specialty-chief-editor-of-the-dermatology-section/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_GniadeckiImage 

Prof. Robert Gniadecki joins 

as Specialty Chief Editor

http://loop.frontiersin.org/people/149508/overview/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_GniadeckiProfile 
Prof. Gniadecki  from the University of Alberta, Canada has joined Frontiers as Specialty Chief Editor of Dermatology in Frontiers in Medicine. He is past President of the Danish Dermatological Society, a board member of the European Society of Dermatological Research and the International Society of Cutaneous Lymphomas.

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/17/frontiers-in-medicine-welcomes-new-specialty-chief-editor-of-the-dermatology-section/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_GniadeckiText 
Learn more here. 

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/04/frontiers-in-medicine-new-leadership-for-the-geriatric-medicine-specialty-section/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_GeriatricImage 

New leadership for the Geriatric Medicine specialty section

http://loop.frontiersin.org/people/183518/overview/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_DwolatzkyProfile 
Dr. Tzvi Dwolatzky  is the new Specialty Chief Editor of the Geriatric Medicine section of 
http://journal.frontiersin.org/journal/medicine/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_Medicine 
Frontiers in Medicine . He has made important scientific contributions on various aspects of aging and common geriatric conditions such as Alzheimer’s disease and other dementias, frailty and longevity.

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/04/frontiers-in-medicine-new-leadership-for-the-geriatric-medicine-specialty-section/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_GeriatricText 
Learn more here. 

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/17/hospitalized-patients-at-risk-if-sodium-levels-are-low/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_GESCI_20160800_61058_MoritzHeroImage 

Frontiers Science Hero:  

Dr. Michael Moritz

Research shows hospitalized patients could be more susceptible to infections if sodium levels are low. 
http://loop.frontiersin.org/people/58603/overview/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_GESCI_20160800_61058_MoritzProfile 
Dr. Michael Moritz , Clinical Director of Pediatric Nephrology at the Children’s Hospital of Pittsburgh of the University of Pittsburgh Medical Center (UPMC), explains more about hyponatremia in this video.

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/17/hospitalized-patients-at-risk-if-sodium-levels-are-low/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_GESCI_20160800_61058_MoritzHeroText 
Watch the video here. 

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/16/frontiers-in-marine-science-looking-for-new-chief-editors/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_MarineBoardImage 

Join the editorial board for 

Frontiers in Marine Science 

http://journal.frontiersin.org/journal/marine-science/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_MarineScience 
Frontiers in Marine Science  is looking for editors for its new section Physical Oceanography and for two existing sections 
http://journal.frontiersin.org/journal/marine-science/section/marine-systematics-and-taxonomy/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_MarineSystematics 
Marine Systematics and Taxonomy , and 
http://journal.frontiersin.org/journal/marine-science/section/ocean-engineering-technology-and-solutions-for-the-blue-economy/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_OceanEngineering 
Ocean Engineering and Technology and Solutions for the Blue Economy .

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/16/frontiers-in-marine-science-looking-for-new-chief-editors/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_CORPO_20160800_61058_MarineBoardText 
Find out more here. 

RESEARCH IN THE NEWS

http://www.cbsnews.com/news/the-hidden-reasons-behind-job-burnout/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FJANY_20160800_61058_CBSNewsImage 

http://www.cbsnews.com/news/the-hidden-reasons-behind-job-burnout/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FJANY_20160800_61058_CBSNewsText 
The hidden reasons behind job burnout 

http://www.ctvnews.ca/health/mediterranean-diet-is-good-brain-food-study-1.3024397/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FJANY_20160800_61058_CTVNewsImage 

http://www.ctvnews.ca/health/mediterranean-diet-is-good-brain-food-study-1.3024397/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FJANY_20160800_61058_CTVNewsText 
Mediterranean diet is good brain food 

http://www.washingtonpost.com/news/animalia/wp/2016/08/05/this-sea-lion-grooves-to-earth-wind-fire-and-science-shows-shes-got-rhythm/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FJANY_20160800_61058_WPimage 

http://www.washingtonpost.com/news/animalia/wp/2016/08/05/this-sea-lion-grooves-to-earth-wind-fire-and-science-shows-shes-got-rhythm/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FJANY_20160800_61058_WPText 
The sea lion grooves to Earth, Wind & Fire - and science show's she's got rhythm 

FEATURED RESEARCH TOPICS

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/06/05/new-interest-sparked-in-the-transportation-of-fluids-in-porous-media/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FRTOP_20160800_61058_PorousMediaImage 

New interest sparked in the transportation of fluids in porous media

Porous media could be at the core of fuel cell technology, providing an alternative to fracking and tar sand exploitation. 
http://loop.frontiersin.org/people/73058/overview/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FRTOP_20160800_61058_HansenProfile 
Prof. Alex Hansen , Field Chief Editor of 
http://journal.frontiersin.org/journal/physics/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FRTOP_20160800_61058_Physics 
Frontiers in Physics , explains how the Frontiers Research Topic 
http://journal.frontiersin.org/researchtopic/3084/flow-and-transformations-in-porous-media/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FRTOP_20160800_61058_PorousMediaRT 
Flow and Transformations in Porous Media  to find solutions.

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/06/05/new-interest-sparked-in-the-transportation-of-fluids-in-porous-media/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FRTOP_20160800_61058_PorousMediaText 
Learn more here. 

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/06/01/conquering-the-great-divide-between-physics-complexity-and-real-world-complexity/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FRTOP_20160800_61058_ComplexityImage 

The great divide between physics complexity and real-world complexity 

How "physics complexity" connects and creates parallels in real-life systems is vastly under-explored. 
http://loop.frontiersin.org/people/28605/overview/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FRTOP_20160800_61058_StruzikProfile 
Prof. Zbigniew R. Struzik  from the University of Tokyo explains how Frontiers Research Topics have helped bring together experts in the field.

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/06/01/conquering-the-great-divide-between-physics-complexity-and-real-world-complexity/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FRTOP_20160800_61058_ComplexityText 
Learn more here. 

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/03/frontiers-ebooks-published-in-july-2016/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FRTOP_20160800_61058_eBooksImage 

Access the latest research by downloading a Frontiers eBook

Did you know all Frontiers eBooks are free to download? We have over 900 eBooks covering topics which are important to today's scientists and the world. Read all the Frontiers' eBooks published in July including "
http://www.frontiersin.org/books/Improving_Animal_Welfare_through_Genetic_Selection/918/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FRTOP_20160800_61058_AnimalWelfareRT 
Improving Animal Welfare through Genetic Selection ".

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/03/frontiers-ebooks-published-in-july-2016/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FRTOP_20160800_61058_eBooksText 
Download our latest eBooks here. 

YOUNG MINDS

http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/16/reject-or-publish-kids-discuss-peer-review/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FYMIN_20160800_61058_KidsPanelImage 

Reject or publish? 

Kids discuss peer-review

Is the paper fact or fiction? Is the paper boring? These are all things this panel of children look for when reviewing a paper for our journal for kids, 
http://kids.frontiersin.org/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FYMIN_20160800_61058_YMHomepage 
Frontiers for Young Minds . Watch the kids explain what criteria should be used when deciding whether a paper should be accepted or not.


 
http://blog.frontiersin.org/2016/08/16/reject-or-publish-kids-discuss-peer-review/?utm_source=F-NLT&utm_medium=EMLF&utm_campaign=MRK_FYMIN_20160800_61058_KidsPanelText 
Watch the video here. 

https://twitter.com/frontiersin 

https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC50TJ2nHy9P1Ca2QMJbAZxw 

https://www.facebook.com/Frontiersin/ 

https://plus.google.com/105250040475367528512/videos 

https://vimeo.com/frontiersin

mailto:community@frontiersin.org 
community@frontiersin.org 

Frontiers SA - EPFL Innovation Park, Building I 1015 Lausanne . Switzerland

body_html:
The latest news from Frontiers
TOP NEWS - AUGUST 2016
Frontiers publishes 50,000th article
Frontiers has reached a major milestone and published its 50,000th article. We are now the home of 57 journals, and the largest editorial board in the world, consisting of over 63,000 researchers from the most prestigious universities. 

Read more here.

Achieving quality at scale: here's how we do it
Publishing 50,000 articles is just the beginning. Dr. Marie Soulière explains how quality at scale is achieved through our unique collaborative peer-review platform. Marie Soulière is the Senior Manager of Peer Review Operations, and of Quality and Ethics at Frontiers.

 Watch the video and read more here.

EMPOWERING & SUPPORTING COMMUNITIES

Science for sustainability:
Editors Summit Highlights 
Over 40 Frontiers editors came together to discuss the future of academic publishing, and how scientists can play a vital role in tackling the global sustainability crisis at our 2016 Frontiers Editors Summit. This video shows all the highlights of the event from great speeches to interesting discussions, all in front of the backdrop of the beautiful Swiss Alps.

Watch the video here.


Prof. Robert Gniadecki joins
as Specialty Chief Editor
Prof. Gniadecki from the University of Alberta, Canada has joined Frontiers as Specialty Chief Editor of Dermatology in Frontiers in Medicine. He is past President of the Danish Dermatological Society, a board member of the European Society of Dermatological Research and the International Society of Cutaneous Lymphomas.

Learn more here.


New leadership for the Geriatric Medicine specialty section
Dr. Tzvi Dwolatzky is the new Specialty Chief Editor of the Geriatric Medicine section of Frontiers in Medicine . He has made important scientific contributions on various aspects of aging and common geriatric conditions such as Alzheimer’s disease and other dementias, frailty and longevity.

Learn more here.


Frontiers Science Hero:  
Dr. Michael Moritz
Research shows hospitalized patients could be more susceptible to infections if sodium levels are low. Dr. Michael Moritz, Clinical Director of Pediatric Nephrology at the Children’s Hospital of Pittsburgh of the University of Pittsburgh Medical Center (UPMC), explains more about hyponatremia in this video.

Watch the video here.


Join the editorial board for
Frontiers in Marine Science 
Frontiers in Marine Science is looking for editors for its new section Physical Oceanography and for two existing sections Marine Systematics and Taxonomy, and Ocean Engineering and Technology and Solutions for the Blue Economy.

Find out more here.


RESEARCH IN THE NEWS
The hidden reasons behind job burnout


Mediterranean diet is good brain food
The sea lion grooves to Earth, Wind & Fire - and science show's she's got rhythm
FEATURED RESEARCH TOPICS
New interest sparked in the transportation of fluids in porous media
Porous media could be at the core of fuel cell technology, providing an alternative to fracking and tar sand exploitation. Prof. Alex Hansen, Field Chief Editor of Frontiers in Physics, explains how the Frontiers Research Topic Flow and Transformations in Porous Media to find solutions.

Learn more here.


The great divide between physics complexity and real-world complexity 
How "physics complexity" connects and creates parallels in real-life systems is vastly under-explored. Prof. Zbigniew R. Struzik from the University of Tokyo explains how Frontiers Research Topics have helped bring together experts in the field.

Learn more here.


Access the latest research by downloading a Frontiers eBook
Did you know all Frontiers eBooks are free to download? We have over 900 eBooks covering topics which are important to today's scientists and the world. Read all the Frontiers' eBooks published in July including "Improving Animal Welfare through Genetic Selection".

Download our latest eBooks here.


YOUNG MINDS
Reject or publish?
Kids discuss peer-review
Is the paper fact or fiction? Is the paper boring? These are all things this panel of children look for when reviewing a paper for our journal for kids, Frontiers for Young Minds. Watch the kids explain what criteria should be used when deciding whether a paper should be accepted or not.

 Watch the video here.

Your feedback is appreciated at community@frontiersin.org

Frontiers SA - EPFL Innovation Park, Building I 1015 Lausanne . Switzerland
